retry_reason: Failure (return NULL) indicates the resource is in DLM_LOCK_RES_DROPPING_REF state and will be freed; retrying the same lookup is pointless until a new resource is created.
contract_summary: Get-wrapper for a DLM lock resource. Acquires a reference via __dlm_lookup_lockres_full (which increments the refcount) and then conditionally drops it if the resource is in dropping state. Returns a pointer to the resource with an acquired reference that the caller must later release with dlm_lockres_put. The caller must check the return value: NULL means the resource is not valid (being freed). The caller must hold dlm->spinlock.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to obtain a lock resource with an acquired reference, conditionally dropping it if the resource is being freed. The only non-refcount logic is a spinlock check on state.
judge.retry_reason: Failure (return NULL) indicates the resource is in DLM_LOCK_RES_DROPPING_REF state and will be freed; retrying the same lookup is pointless until a new resource is created.

check.reason: The function __dlm_lookup_lockres returns a pointer to a dlm_lock_resource with a reference already taken by the callee __dlm_lookup_lockres_full. The access path res.refs.refcount correctly traces from the returned variable to the refcount field, and the type chain matches the expected struct layout. Although the actual increment happens in the callee, the net effect at the function interface is a get on the return value.
